title: boolean zoo (nand/xor/implies + andalso/orelse)
manager: The full set of boolean operators — not just and/or/not but nand, xor, and implies, plus short-circuiting andalso/orelse — because machine guards are logic, and having `implies` as a first-class operator lets a contract read like the proposition it actually is instead of a de Morgan puzzle you re-derive every time. The short-circuit forms give you control over evaluation order when a guard has a side condition or an expensive test.
cs: and/or/not/nand/xor/implies + short-circuit andalso/orelse; total boolean algebra; feeds guards (W6.61), contracts (W6.62-.64), and quantifiers (W6.42).
